steps.title: Recognize what the constant term represents
steps.content: In any function h(t), when we set t = 0, all terms containing t vanish and only the constant term remains. Since t = 0 represents the launch moment, the constant term gives the initial height. In $h(t) = -16t^2 + 110t + 72$, the constant term is 72.

steps.title: Evaluate h(0) to find the initial height
steps.content:
$$h(0) = -16(0)^2 + 110(0) + 72 = 72 \text{ feet}$$
At the moment of launch (t = 0), the object is at a height of 72 feet above the ground. This is the initial height of the object. That matches choice A — A is correct.
